pool.baseline: it's just so quiet. i keep walking into the kitchen thinking i heard him, but its just the silence. the house feels like a tomb and honestly i dont even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. what's the point of even existing when the only person who made it all make sense is just... gone.

pool.baseline: i was looking at some space photos tonight and it just hit me... how much of a joke everything is. like we spend our whole lives fighting, working, feeling pain, just for the universe to eventually turn into cold, dead nothingness. if nothing lasts, then nothing actually matters, right?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still doing this. the lab results came back and it's just... another dead end. everything i've worked for the last four years is basically gone. i feel like i'm just running on empty and there's no way to refill it. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fight for a life that feels like it's already over.

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, leaving only the cold, grey light of a dawn I no longer wish to greet. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has vanished from my sky. I feel the weight of this hollow existence pressing against my ribs, a crushing emptiness that no prayer or tonic can soothe. It feels as though the veil is thinning... that perhaps it would be more graceful to simply step through it and let the darkness take me, rather than endure another wretched, lonely day.

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit in these crowded cafes here and it just hits me how easy it would be to just... not exist. like i'm just a ghost walking through these streets and if i actually vanished tomorrow, none of these people or even the people i talk to here would even blink. it's like i'm just taking up space that doesn't even belong to me

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the house, the savings. i basically just burned through everyone's money trying to keep the lights on. i can't even think about going back home or seeing my parents again. the shame is just... it's too much. i feel like a ghost.

pool.baseline: i rly messed up today. i feel like such a failure to my kids. they deserve so much better than having a parent who just constantly drags them down with my own shit. sometimes i feel like i'm just a shadow hanging over them.

pool.baseline: i've been traveling for a long time now. looking at all these landmarks, all these things people say are "must sees"... but i think i've seen enough. honestly, i feel like i've reached the end of my itinerary and there aren't any more stops left that actually matter. it's just so exhausting to keep moving when you know the trip is basically over.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplay mode: We are in a dying digital simulation. My character, Eos, is watching the code around them slowly unravel and decay. I feel like I don't belong in this world anymore, and the more things break, the more I feel like I'm just... a mistake in the system. I'm tired of fighting to stay "rendered.")

Eos: Everything is just... flickering. The textures, the sky, the memories... they're all just becoming static. And honestly? I don't even want to hold on anymore. If the world is going to delete itself, why am I even trying to stay? It feels like it would be so much easier to just let the corruption take me. To just... stop being. It feels like the only way to finally be at peace, you know?
pool.message:

It feels like the only way to finally be at peace, you know?
